---
title: "Module 1: Introduction to Python"
---

## Module Overview

Welcome to Module 1! This module introduces you to Python programming and covers the fundamental concepts you'll need to start writing Python code.

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, you will be able to:

- Install and set up Python on your computer
- Use Python interactive shell and write Python scripts
- Understand and use different data types (integers, floats, strings, booleans)
- Create and manipulate variables
- Perform basic arithmetic and logical operations
- Understand operator precedence and type conversion

## Module Contents

### Lesson 1: Setting Up Python
Learn how to install Python, set up your development environment, and write your first Python program.

### Lesson 2: Variables and Data Types
Understand how to store and work with different types of data in Python.

### Lesson 3: Basic Operations
Master arithmetic, comparison, and logical operations in Python.

## Prerequisites

No prior programming experience is required for this module!

::: {.callout-tip}
## Getting the Most Out of This Module

- Follow along with the code examples
- Type out the code yourself rather than copying and pasting
- Experiment by modifying the examples
- Complete all exercises before checking solutions
- Take breaks if you feel overwhelmed
:::

## Estimated Time

This module should take approximately 4-6 hours to complete, including exercises and practice time.

## Ready to Start?

Begin with [Lesson 1: Setting Up Python](lesson1.qmd)!
